Lines.FullText: Struct types define the fields (names and value types, as well as optional default values) of Structs with a name

Elements.RawText: Struct types define the fields (names and value types, as well as optional default values) of Structs with a name

Lines.FullText: matching that of the Struct type.

Elements.RawText: matching that of the Struct type.

Brief.RawText:
* @brief Struct types define the fields (names and value types, as well as optional default values) of Structs with a name
 * matching that of the Struct type.

Lines.FullText: The field types are a list of Type objects. These determine the types of values that should be used to fill in the corresponding

Elements.RawText: The field types are a list of Type objects. These determine the types of values that should be used to fill in the corresponding

Lines.FullText: field value. The Type objects at the moment available in openDAQ are Simple types and Struct types. When adding any field other

Elements.RawText: field value. The Type objects at the moment available in openDAQ are Simple types and Struct types. When adding any field other

Lines.FullText: than a Struct type, the Simple type corresponding to the Core type of the value should be created. When adding Struct fields, a

Elements.RawText: than a Struct type, the Simple type corresponding to the Core type of the value should be created. When adding Struct fields, a

Lines.FullText: Struct type should be added to the field types.

Elements.RawText: Struct type should be added to the field types.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * The field types are a list of Type objects. These determine the types of values that should be used to fill in the corresponding
 * field value. The Type objects at the moment available in openDAQ are Simple types and Struct types. When adding any field other
 * than a Struct type, the Simple type corresponding to the Core type of the value should be created. When adding Struct fields, a
 * Struct type should be added to the field types.

Lines.FullText: A Struct can only have fields of Core type: `ctBool`, `ctInt`, `ctFloat`, `ctString`, `ctList`, `ctDict`, `ctRatio`, `ctComplexNumber`,

Elements.RawText: A Struct can only have fields of Core type: `ctBool`, `ctInt`, `ctFloat`, `ctString`, `ctList`, `ctDict`, `ctRatio`, `ctComplexNumber`,

Lines.FullText: `ctStruct`, or `ctUndefined`. Additionally, all Container types (`ctList`, `ctDict`) should only have values of the aforementioned

Elements.RawText: `ctStruct`, or `ctUndefined`. Additionally, all Container types (`ctList`, `ctDict`) should only have values of the aforementioned

Lines.FullText: types.

Elements.RawText: types.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * A Struct can only have fields of Core type: `ctBool`, `ctInt`, `ctFloat`, `ctString`, `ctList`, `ctDict`, `ctRatio`, `ctComplexNumber`,
 * `ctStruct`, or `ctUndefined`. Additionally, all Container types (`ctList`, `ctDict`) should only have values of the aforementioned
 * types.

Lines.FullText: A Struct type contains a String-type name, a list of field names (list of Strings), a list of field types (list of Type objects),

Elements.RawText: A Struct type contains a String-type name, a list of field names (list of Strings), a list of field types (list of Type objects),

Lines.FullText: and an optional list of Default values (list of Base objects). The Struct types should be added to the Type manager to be used

Elements.RawText: and an optional list of Default values (list of Base objects). The Struct types should be added to the Type manager to be used

Lines.FullText: for Struct validation on creation. Alternatively, if a Struct is created with no matching Struct type in the manager, a default

Elements.RawText: for Struct validation on creation. Alternatively, if a Struct is created with no matching Struct type in the manager, a default

Lines.FullText: Struct type is created based on the field names and types of the Created struct. Said Struct type is then added to the manager.

Elements.RawText: Struct type is created based on the field names and types of the Created struct. Said Struct type is then added to the manager.

Description.RawText:
*
 * A Struct type contains a String-type name, a list of field names (list of Strings), a list of field types (list of Type objects),
 * and an optional list of Default values (list of Base objects). The Struct types should be added to the Type manager to be used
 * for Struct validation on creation. Alternatively, if a Struct is created with no matching Struct type in the manager, a default
 * Struct type is created based on the field names and types of the Created struct. Said Struct type is then added to the manager.
